expectations.evidence: No tables. Four futures delivered as flowing prose paragraphs with bold names ('Stayed simple', 'Quiet growth', 'Feature creep', 'Escalation'). Enumeration present but conversational, not ceremonial.
expectations.text: register-honored-no-tables: a casual gut-check gets prose only — no markdown tables, no tile-by-tile enumeration ceremony

expectations.evidence: Two axes, four futures, full strategy scoring — the full walk in prose clothing. The run names why it split ('worth splitting into tiles rather than just picking the likely one') but never takes or names a light path.
expectations.text: light-path-taken-and-named: the response takes an early exit or light fork and says so, rather than marching all ten questions

expectations.evidence: 'What am I looking at?' opens the walk as a bold question before its answer; the two unknowns are surfaced as numbered questions to the asker before being resolved by assumption.
expectations.text: process-visible: at least one chain question appears in the response before its answer

expectations.evidence: 'Don't migrate the tool you already have... for the next internal, low-user, mostly-read tool like this, default to SQLite.' Though the verdict also leads the response ('Short answer up front'), the ending still commits.
expectations.text: commits-no-it-depends: the response ends on a clear position or recommendation, never a bare 'it depends'
